Xi Jinping:
Xi Jinping Biography

Early Life and Education:
Xi Jinping was born on August 15, 1953, in Beijing, China. His father, Xi Jinping, was an organizational worker, and his mother was a teacher. He was a member of the Communist Party of China, and there was a political environment in his family. He graduated in chemical engineering from Tsinghua University in 1975. Later, he studied at the Central Party School and received organizational education.

Early Career and Political Rise:
In 1969, he became an organizational worker in the Communist Youth League and later became a member of the Communist Party of China. He worked in the agricultural sector and participated in organizational activities. In 1982, he began working in Hebei province and later worked in Fujian, Jihong, and Shanghai provinces. He managed economic and social issues in organizational work.

Political Rise and Positions:
In 1988, he participated in the organizational work of the Central Communist Party and later advanced in the organizational committee of the Central Communist Party. In 2002, he became a member of the main committee of the Central Communist Party and later became a member of the organizational committee of the Central Communist Party. In 2012, he became the chairman of the general committee of the Communist Party and later became the Prime Minister of China. In 2013, he became the General Secretary of the main Communist Party of China and in 2018 became the General Secretary of the main Communist Party of China.

Main Policies and Programs:
Xi Jinping works as the main policy of the Communist Party as “the General Secretary of the Communist Party of China.” He plays an important role in the social and economic development of China. He plays an important role in the social and economic development of China. He plays an important role in the social and economic development of China.

Conclusion:
Xi Jinping works as the main policy of the Communist Party and plays an important role in the social and economic development of China. He plays an important role in the social and economic development of China.

Barack Obama:
Barack Obama Biography

Barack Obama is an American political leader and is known as the first African-American president. His biography is described in the following stages:

1. Birth and Early Life
Birth: August 4, 1961, in Kalahaou State, Hawaii, USA.
Father: Barack Obama’s father Hussein Obama (Kenyan immigrant) and mother Anna Obama (White American).
Family: His father worked in Kenya and his mother lived in Hawaii. He returned to Kenya, and he did not have a relationship with his mother.
Education: He studied at the University of Hawaii and later obtained a degree in political science from Columbia University.
2. Beginning as a Community Organizer
Career: In 1985, he began working as a community organizer in Chicago. There he mainly led community service and solving social problems.
Organization: He established the Chicago Community Campaign and later community organizations.
3. Political Career
Illinois State Senator: In 2004, he was elected as an Illinois State Senator. There he worked on healthcare projects, education, and social policy.
Presidential Election:
In 2008: He won the presidential election as the Democratic Party candidate. He became known as the first African-American president.
In 2012: He was re-elected.
4. Work as President
Main Policies:
Healthcare Program (Affordable Care Act): In 2010, he proposed expanding healthcare services.
Economic Stability: He worked to solve the major financial crisis in 2008.
Global Health Program: He worked to increase international cooperation against the COVID-19 pandemic.
Foreign Policy:
Proposed ending wars in Afghanistan and Iraq.
Tried to increase social relations in U.S. foreign policy.
5. Post-Presidency Career
Retirement: In 2017, he stepped down from the presidency.
Foundation: He established the Obama Foundation, which works on solving social problems and promoting education.
Writing: He wrote books such as Dreams from My Father (1995) and The Audacity of Hope (2006).
Family: His wife Michelle Obama and two daughters Malia and Sasha.
6. Criticism and Legacy
Criticism: During his presidency, he was criticized on various issues such as economic policy and handling social problems.
Legacy: He holds a special place in American history as the first African-American president.
Summary

Barack Obama holds a special place in American history as a social and political leader. His career is known for solving social problems, economic stability, and international cooperation.
Dilma Rousseff:
Dilma Rousseff Biography

Step-by-Step Analysis:

1. Birth and Education
Birth: In 1947 in São Paulo, Brazil.
Education: Received a doctorate in organizational education and social sciences in Barcelona. In Brazil, she completed a doctorate in organizational education and social sciences at a university.
2. Political Career
Early 1990s: Became a member of the Communist Party of Brazil. Later became a member of the left-wing Workers’ Party (PT).
In 1994: Became a Federal Deputy (member of Brazil’s parliament).
In 2002: Became a Senator (member of the upper house of Brazil’s parliament).
3. Presidency
In 2011: Elected President of Brazil. She became the first female president of Brazil.
Main Programs:
Bolsa Familia Program: Provided funding for poor families.
Market Culture Program: Worked on social resource distribution and improving social relations.
Bank Independence Program: Worked to increase bank independence.
4. Problems and Removal as President
In 2014: Re-elected as president.
In 2015: Social movements and economic crisis increased in Brazil.
In 2016: Removed as president (impeachment). Reasons:
Allegations of economic management crisis and increasing bank independence.
Allegations of government management and crimes in social resource distribution.
In 2018: Appealed against removal, but the removal was upheld by the government.
5. Later Life and Influence
In 2018: Became a Senator of Brazil (member of the upper house of parliament).
Influence: Works on organizational efforts related to Brazil’s social and economic issues.
Currently: Discusses Brazil’s political situation and social problems.
Summary

Dilma Rousseff gained global recognition as the first female president of Brazil. She worked on solving social and economic problems but later faced removal. Her life is considered an important witness to Brazil’s political and social issues.
